steps.title: Identify what needs demonstrating
steps.content: The claim that the narrator was frightened signals we need evidence of his emotional fear, not just observation of the sailor's behavior or appearance.

steps.title: Match the fear signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D — "How [the sailor] haunted my dreams, I need scarcely tell you. On stormy nights, when the wind shook the four corners of the house and the surf roared along the cove and up the cliffs, I would see him in a thousand forms, and with a thousand diabolical expressions." directly conveys fear through vivid nightmare imagery—the sailor “haunted” the narrator's dreams with “diabolical expressions,” showing raw emotional distress.

The alternatives describe the sailor's arrival, his quiet demeanor, or his unusual appearance without expressing fear.

The nightmare imagery is correct.
